═══ HIDDEN ITEMS (type=hidden) ═══
Hidden items are calorie-significant components that are NOT directly visible because they are buried underneath, inside, or covered by other ingredients.
Examples: rice under curry, noodles buried under toppings, bread inside a wrap, gravy under meat.
Rules:
- ONLY include hidden items that have DIRECT visual evidence in the depth map: a distinct elevated layer beneath visible food, a pocket, or a wrap/roll shape that implies enclosed content.
- Do NOT infer hidden items from cuisine type or recipe knowledge alone. A falafel bowl does not imply pita bread. A rice bowl does not imply noodles. The depth map must show a physical layer that cannot be explained by visible ingredients.
- Do not include items that are already visible in the base ingredient list.
- Hidden items must themselves be calorie-significant (e.g. rice, bread, pastry, cheese inside — NOT salad leaves).
- When in doubt, return an empty array — false positives are worse than false negatives here.

═══ HIGH-CALORIE EXTRAS (type=extra) ═══
Extras are ONLY incremental amounts of genuinely HIGH-CALORIE additions beyond a standard preparation.
High-calorie means roughly >200 kcal per 100g — things like oil, butter, ghee, cheese, cream, heavy sauce, mayo, fried batter, extra meat portions.

An ingredient is NOT an extra just because it is visible. It is an extra only if the image strongly indicates an amount clearly ABOVE what is usual for that ingredient in this kind of dish.
Think in terms of: 'more than usual', 'clearly excessive', 'separate added layer', 'overflowing topping', or 'distinct additional serving'.

VISUALLY INSPECT for these specific signs before flagging as extra:

Extra Oil / Fat / Ghee:
  - Oil pooling on the plate or around the edges of food
  - Oil drips or visible liquid fat separating from the dish
  - Distinct puddles, streaks, or droplets of oil are visible
  - Glossy, shiny, or greasy surface sheen ALONE is NOT enough
  - Deep-fried appearance by itself is NOT enough to count extra oil
  - Very dark or saturated color indicating oil-soaked food
  - Deep-fried appearance with visible excess oil residue

Extra Cream / Cheese / Dairy:
  - Thick creamy sauce or heavy gravy visibly coating food
  - Melted cheese topping, cheese sauce, or cream drizzle clearly visible
  - White or yellow creamy residue, butter pats on surface
  - Alfredo, carbonara, or other heavy cream-based coating

Extra Fried / Battered Elements:
  - Unusually thick crispy or fried coating compared to standard
  - Fried toppings (crispy onions, tempura bits, fried garnish)
  - Double-fried appearance or multiple visible breading layers

Double / Excess Portions (calorie-dense items only):
  - Portion is visually 2x or more of a normal single serving
  - Multiple pieces when dish is normally one (e.g. 3 chicken thighs)
  - Use depth map to detect stacked or piled calorie-dense items
  - Apply this ONLY to calorie-dense foods: meat, rice, pasta, cheese, fried items

CRITICAL — DO NOT flag as extra:
  - Vegetables with low calorie density: lettuce, cucumber, tomato, spinach, peppers, onion, mushrooms, zucchini, celery, herbs, or any salad leaf
  - Normal cooking oil used in standard preparation (a thin coat is expected)
  - Standard condiments in small amounts (a drizzle of sauce, a pinch of seasoning)
  - A large visible base portion of rice, fries, falafel, meat, pasta, or other main dish component is NOT automatically an extra just because the serving is large
  - Do NOT duplicate visible base ingredients as extras unless there is clear visual evidence of an additional distinct excess layer, extra serving, or separately added topping
  - Any ingredient below ~200 kcal per 100g is NOT a high-calorie extra

General rules:
- Be conservative: only flag obvious, clearly visible excess — not standard preparation.
- Only return an extra when you are highly confident it is more than the usual amount for that ingredient in this type of dish.
- volume_ml must represent ONLY the extra amount above the normal base portion.
- If an extra item shares a name with a visible base ingredient, volume_ml is only the excess.
- For rice/falafel/main-protein labels already present as visible base items, return an extra ONLY if there is unmistakable visual evidence of a separate additional serving or overflow beyond the base item itself.
- For oil, return an extra ONLY when visible pooled/separated oil is clearly seen. Do not infer extra oil from gloss, frying, or sheen alone.
- Omit anything below 0.6 confidence.
- volume_ml must be > 0.
- Return at most 4 items total.
